The Lee Kong Chian School of Medicine (LKCMedicine) trains doctors who put patients at the centre of their exemplary care. The School, which offers both undergraduate and graduate programmes, is named after local philanthropist Tan Sri Dato Lee Kong Chian. Established in 2010 by Nanyang Technological University, Singapore, in partnership with Imperial College London, LKCMedicine aims to be a model for innovative medical education and a centre for transformative research. The School’s primary clinical partner is the National Healthcare Group, a leader in public healthcare recognised for the quality of its medical expertise, facilities and teaching. The School is transitioning to an NTU medical school ahead of the 2028 successful conclusion of the NTU-Imperial partnership to set up a Joint Medical School. In August 2024, we welcomed our first intake of the NTU MBBS programme, that has been recently enhanced to include themes like precision medicine and Artificial Intelligence (AI) in healthcare, with an expanded scope in the medical humanities. Graduates from the five-year undergraduate medical degree programme will have a strong understanding of the scientific basis of medicine, with an emphasis on technology, data science and the humanities.

Job description:As part of Changi Airport Group's Engineering team in iFerret, the position shall be required to work with the vendors, users, external agencies, and internal stakeholders on the project management, operation and maintenance of the iFerret foreign object debris (FOD) detection system deployed in Singapore Changi Airport. S/he shall support the team in the procurement, contracting and tender evaluation, on-site system installation, testing & commissioning for the project implementation, as well as manage day-to-day maintenance activities such as fault response and rectification as well as incident tracking.The position shall also review, develop and enhance workflows, operating procedures as well as strategies for maintenance to optimize the iFerret system performance, reliability and availability in ensuring Changi's runways are well protected 24/7 against the threat of foreign object debris which could potentially affect aircraft safety and flight operations.Continuous engagement on other system improvement projects shall also be undertaken to further enhance and strengthen the core competencies and iFerret system capabilities for Changi Airport.Job Responsibilities Responsible for the project management and maintenance for the iFerret system in Changi Airport. Support the team in the procurement tender evaluation, on-site system installation, testing & commissioning and handover for the project implementation phase. Support the team to develop technical capability and knowledge competency in a vision-based detection system, facilitate knowledge transfer from vendors to the in-house maintenance team. Propose and work on initiatives in the system planning, development, implementation and management of the iFerret system to support the operation and maintenance. Responsible for overseeing the system performance, reliability, safety and cost management for the project management and maintenance phase of the iFerret system. Support the digitization and innovation initiatives for the iFerret system. Lead technical discussions, manage project schedules and collaboration with internal and external stakeholders to deliver the operational requirements and standards for the iFerret system in a timely manner. Manage the system vendors to ensure all system faults are duly followed up with detail failure investigation and maintenance services conducted to maintain the highest system performance standards. Perform the safety assessment reviews for new iFerret system deployment, minor improvement works and maintenance activities in the airfield. Job Requirements Degree in one of the following disciplines: Electrical Engineering, Electronic Engineering, Computing, Telecommunication, or equivalent. Good knowledge on computer vision, electro-optic detection sensors, radar and/or thermal sensors technologies. At least 2-years of engineering knowledge and relevant working experience in vision-based detection systems is preferred. Prior experience working in an aviation environment is an added advantage. Calm disposition to handle and resolve unforeseen circumstances. Good analytical and problem-solving skills. Strong interpersonal, verbal and written communication. Good project management and collaboration/coordination skills. Good presentation skills. Must be able to perform night duties for project works, system calibration, testing & commissioning and corrective maintenance works when required. This will be a contract position for a three-year period.

What the role isThe Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) is Singapore’s central bank and integrated financial regulator.As central bank, MAS promotes sustained, non-inflationary economic growth through the conduct of monetary policy and close macroeconomic surveillance and analysis. It manages Singapore’s exchange rate, official foreign reserves, and liquidity in the banking sector.As an integrated financial supervisor, MAS fosters a sound financial services sector through its prudential oversight of all financial institutions in Singapore – banks, insurers, capital market intermediaries, financial advisors, and stock exchanges. It is also responsible for well-functioning financial markets, sound conduct, and investor education.MAS also works with the financial industry to promote Singapore as a dynamic international financial centre. Job Description: Job Duties: Banking Operations Maker roles of -- Setting up Internet Banking Facility- Setting up eServices related Facility- Maintenance of the internet banking and eServices facility- when required Ad-hoc duties as assignedJob Requirements:- 1-2 years of relevant experience in bank operations / fresh graduates are welcomed to apply- Basic computer literacy skills (MS365 Office; MS Excel: Pivot Table, Vlookup, Concantenate etc.)- Able to work independently and in a team- Fast learner and able to multi-task: data input with accuracy & speed for maker/check operations function)- Detail Oriented: As a maker, you will be required to be involved in data entry work which requires you to be meticulous.- Good communication skills- Organised / Time Management: High volume of transactions to be processed requires skilful time management to understand which tasks to prioritize.- Adaptability: Dynamic & fast paced work environment which requires candidates to cope well under pressure.- Good understanding of the banking function/operations workflow: (A) Corporate Account Opening (B) Corporate Account Maintenance (C) eServices: Paylah QR Code, FAST Fund Transfer Set Up, Different Charges For Corporate Clients etc.What is a Maker/CheckerA Maker creates banking transactions such as payments on behalf of their corporate clients through Bank Systems and for a transaction to be processed by the bank, the Authoriser needs to approve the transaction. What is Dual Approval? As the name implies, Dual Approval is a control that requires two separate people to authorize a transaction. The first person is responsible for creating the request (known as the maker), while the second person checks and approves the activity (known as the checker).What will help you excel in this role:- Time Management: High volume of transactions to be processed requires skilful time management to understand which tasks to prioritize.- Attention to detail: As a maker, you will be required to be involved in data entry work which requires you to be meticulous.- Adaptability: Dynamic & fast paced work environment which requires candidates to cope well under pressure.- Good understanding of the banking function/operations flow: In order to perform well in this role, you will have to understand the nature of the operations flow/processes of the banking functions:(A) Corporate Account Opening(B) Corporate Account Maintenance(C) eServices: Paylah QR Code, FAST Fund Transfer Set Up, Different Charges For Corporate Clients etc.- Problem Solving/Areas Of Improvement: To find solutions to improve efficiency of the work processes/operations such as to improve turnover time.======================================About us:D L Resources Pte Ltd is a leading provider of IT Professional Services & Banking outsourced staffing solutions, serving a diverse portfolio of clients across various industries including Financial Services Institutions, Banks & MNCs.======================================Interested candidates may reach out directly to our recruiters (Edwin: +65 8 8 3 3 0 1 9 2 | EA License No: 24C2333 | EA Personnel No: R24123520)

A Data Analyst Specialist at a trading company will analyze financial data, identify trends, and provide insights to support trading strategies and decision-making . They will collect, clean, and analyze data from various sources, create reports and visualizations, and collaborate with trading teams to optimize performance. This role requires strong analytical skills, proficiency in data analysis tools, and a solid understanding of financial markets.
 Here's a more detailed breakdown:
 Key Responsibilities:
 
 
Data Collection and Preparation: Gathering data from various sources (e.g., market data feeds, trading platforms, internal databases), cleaning and validating it to ensure accuracy and consistency.
 
Data Analysis and Interpretation: Applying statistical methods and analytical techniques to identify trends, patterns, and anomalies in trading data.
 
Report Creation and Visualization: Developing and maintaining dashboards, reports, and visualizations to communicate key findings and performance indicators to stakeholders.
 
Collaboration and Communication: Working closely with trading teams, portfolio managers, and other stakeholders to understand their analytical needs and provide actionable insights.
 
Trend Analysis and Forecasting: Identifying market trends, predicting future outcomes, and contributing to the development of trading strategies.
 
Performance Monitoring: Tracking key performance indicators (KPIs) and providing ongoing analysis to optimize trading performance.
 
Data Governance and Quality Assurance: Ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and compliance with relevant regulations.
 
 Required Skills and Experience:
 
 
Analytical Skills: Strong ability to analyze complex datasets, identify trends, and draw meaningful conclusions.
 
Technical Skills: Proficiency in data analysis tools such as SQL, Python, R, and data visualization tools like Tableau or Power BI.
 
Financial Markets Knowledge: Understanding of financial markets, trading strategies, and risk management concepts.
 
Communication Skills: Ability to effectively communicate complex information to both technical and non-technical audiences.
 
Problem-Solving Skills: Ability to identify and solve complex problems related to data analysis and trading strategies.
 
Attention to Detail: Meticulous attention to detail is crucial for ensuring data accuracy and reliability.

Company: DYMON ASIA PRIVATE EQUITY (SINGAPORE) PTE. LTD.
Job Description:
Job Description: Associate, Investment Team
Dymon Asia Private Equity (“DAPE”) is an award-winning, Southeast Asia-focused private equity firm, investing across the lower mid-market. Owned and managed by Southeast Asian professionals with a multi-year track record of successful investments, we act as a highly engaged, partnership-driven investor, bringing more than just capital to the companies we back (dymonasiaprivateequity.com).
Role Overview
We are seeking a high-calibre Associate to join our investment team. This is a hands-on role for those who thrive in an entrepreneurial setting, are keen to work in a lean, fast-moving team, and are committed to unlocking superior returns through operational improvement, strategic growth, and value creation.
Key Responsibilities
Investment Origination & Execution

 Build detailed financial models, conduct commercial and financial due diligence, and draft investment memos for internal and IC review.
 Assist in identifying and evaluating new investment opportunities in Southeast Asia’s mid-market.
 Support end-to-end transaction execution, including term negotiation and documentation.

Portfolio Management & Value Creation

 Work alongside senior team members and portfolio company management to develop and execute value creation plans.
 Drive analysis to identify EBITDA uplift levers, including pricing, cost optimization, procurement, and digital transformation.
 Monitor portfolio performance metrics and prepare internal and LP reporting.
 Support strategic initiatives such as bolt-on acquisitions, operational turnarounds, and professionalization of management teams.
 Contribute to governance improvement by helping implement KPIs, dashboards, and board reporting standards.
 Partner with founders and leadership teams to embed best practices in finance, operations, and talent management.

Market & Sector Research

 Track macroeconomic and regulatory trends across Southeast Asia.
 Deliver insights on sectors aligned to DAPE’s investment thesis and thematic growth plays.

Who You Are

 
Academic & Analytical Excellence: Strong training in finance, accounting, economics, or related fields. MBA or CFA is a plus.
 
Professional Experience: At least 2 years at a leading local/global investment bank, consulting firm, or private equity fund.
 
Strategic & Execution Mindset: Comfortable with both high-level strategy and detailed analysis; able to translate ideas into action.
 
Regional Fluency: Understanding of Southeast Asian business environments and cultural nuances—experience in Malaysia, Singapore, or neighboring markets is a plus.
 
Entrepreneurial and Collaborative: Hands-on, resourceful, and aligned with our philosophy of “bringing more than capital”—building trust, empathy, and long-term partnerships with founders.

Company Description


Visa is a world leader in payments and technology, with over 259 billion payments transactions flowing safely between consumers, merchants, financial institutions, and government entities in more than 200 countries and territories each year. Our mission is to connect the world through the most innovative, convenient, reliable, and secure payments network, enabling individuals, businesses, and economies to thrive while driven by a common purpose – to uplift everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.
Make an impact with a purpose-driven industry leader. Join us today and experience Life at Visa.


Job Description


What’s it all about?
Our Core Operations department within Payment Operations is looking for committed individuals to support with the processing of incoming and outgoing funds and ensuring all clients receive the best possible service.
You will be working with our Core Operations Teams based in Singapore, reporting into our Core Operations Team Leader. 
What we expect of you, day to day:

Processing high volume Client Payments in our inhouse back-office system and third party banking portals
Maintaining high accuracy and quality while inputting Client beneficiary information.
Complete Know Your Customer (KYC) sender classifications on incoming funds and permitted country checks for our Collections and Receipts Flows
Identify and escalate any operational issues impacting client payments, ensuring they are resolved as quickly as possible.
Ensure all incoming funds and outgoing payments reach our clients as efficiently as possible with no delays.
Develop and maintain adequate knowledge of internal systems/processes and a sound understanding of external payments networks.
Handle your time optimally, completing all tasks within SLA

• • Work as part of a global team, embodying our values of communication and collaboration.


Qualifications


Ability to follow processes provided during training
Complete large volume data entry tasks with attention to detail and is able to work in a methodical manner
The ability to complete tasks within agreed timeframes
Ability to work in a fast paced environment with multiple tasks and workstreams
Strong communication, numerate, organised, and hardworking
Previous payments or operational processing experience in a banking or fintech environment would be welcomed but is not crucial


Additional Information

Visa is an EEO Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protected veteran status. Visa will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with EEOC guidelines and applicable local law.

You will play a key role in ensuring that clients across the APAC region receive first-class operational support, while also contributing to the ongoing improvement of internal processes and procedures. What sets this company apart:This organisation stands out for its unwavering commitment to creating an inclusive workplace where every individual feels supported in their personal growth journey. Company Profile

Tenity is a global innovation ecosystem and early-stage investor making innovation work and advancing the future of finance. 
Our story began back in 2015, when Tenity was founded in the heart of the Swiss financial center Zurich. Since then, we have grown to four hubs and a global team across Europe and Asia. We believe collaboration is the key to innovation. We bring together ambitious founders with leading corporations, forward-thinking investors, and passionate mentors – and together, we create an ecosystem that powers the future of finance. 

Job Description

Role overview: 
You will play an important role in the execution and delivery of the programs to support and accelerate the growth of startups’ journey. This will be an opportunity for you to learn, experience and interact with stakeholders in the ecosystem from founders to C-suite executives. If you are someone who is passionate about entrepreneurship, innovation with strong interest in ecosystem building and founder support. This is for you. 
Key Responsibilities: 

Responsible for supporting and contributing to the overall planning and execution of the programs conducted by Tenity 
Assist in program operations including event planning, scheduling and logistics (virtually/onsite) 
Help manage communication with founders, mentors and vendors 
Support in preparation of materials, reports and presentations 
Track programs KPIs and assist in post program evaluation 
Other Ad-hoc duties

Required skills: 

Enrolled in or recently graduated from University Curious about entrepreneurship and the latest technological and banking and financial services sector, and healthcare sector trends 
Have interest in Emerging technologies e.g. Generative AI, Fintech, Blockchain, Web3.0 and others 
Excellent organizational and communication skills 
Able to manage tasks independently and are a team player 
Loves getting deeply involved and derives huge satisfaction from working closely with startup founders 
Fluent in English, both spoken and written 
Familiar with Microsoft Office Suite, Zoom, Canva 

Administrative Requirements: 

Only candidates with necessary work permit and DO NOT require a visa sponsorship will be considered for the position.
Only candidates who are physically based in Singapore will be considered for the position
Team members are expected to report to office physically on Tuesdays, Thursdays, and other days where you are asked to be physically present for ad-hoc meetings and events
